market_note: Stablecoin market cap hits a record ~$322B, now exceeding the FX reserves of 95 countries, as issuers race to comply with the GENIUS Act's July 18 rulemaking deadline.

stablecoin_items.headline: Stripe, Visa, BlackRock back new OUSD stablecoin to rival Tether and Circle
stablecoin_items.summary: Open Standard, a coalition of 140+ businesses, launched Open USD (OUSD) on Solana, returning reserve yield to businesses instead of the issuer. Circle shares slipped 13% on the news; Tether's CEO called it 'Player 2.'

stablecoin_items.headline: Revolut to delist USDT in Europe by August 31 over MiCA non-compliance
stablecoin_items.summary: Tether has not sought MiCA licensing, forcing Revolut to drop USDT from EU listings while Circle's licensed USDC keeps its access, per MiCA rules effective July 1.

stablecoin_items.headline: GENIUS Act stablecoin rules due July 18 as agencies race final rulemaking
stablecoin_items.summary: OCC, FDIC, Treasury, FinCEN, OFAC and the Fed must finalize stablecoin issuer rules by the one-year statutory deadline; comment periods closed June 9 with the Fed's proposal still outstanding.

stablecoin_items.headline: Circle expands USDC access via Standard Chartered, mints $250M on Solana
stablecoin_items.summary: Circle enabled institutional USDC minting and redemption for Standard Chartered clients and minted an additional 250 million USDC on Solana this week.

hack_items.headline: H1 2026 crypto hacks hit record 207 incidents, $972M stolen
hack_items.summary: Despite more frequent attacks, total losses fell versus prior years; North Korea-linked groups accounted for roughly 66% ($643M) of all funds stolen in the first half of 2026.

hack_items.headline: Drift Protocol exploit remains the largest hack of 2026 at ~$285-295M
hack_items.summary: A Lazarus Group social-engineering campaign against Drift Protocol drained roughly $285-295 million, the single biggest crypto theft so far this year.

hack_items.headline: KelpDAO loses $293M in cross-chain bridge exploit
hack_items.summary: Attackers exploited a LayerZero-based bridge flaw to drain 116,500 rsETH from Kelp DAO, ranking among 2026's largest DeFi exploits.

hack_items.headline: Mystery attack drains hundreds of EVM wallets, root cause still unknown
hack_items.summary: On-chain investigator ZachXBT flagged an ongoing cross-chain wallet-draining campaign that has taken over $107K via suspected MetaMask phishing emails; no confirmed exploit vector yet.
